items.title: Negative regulation of the NF-κB pathway by the ubiquitin ligase Nedd4-1(NE)
items.author: Daniela Rotin
items.content: TNFα-induced NF-κB activation upregulates Nedd4-1(NE), a primate-specific ubiquitin ligase that regulates IKKβ and NF-κB1 to suppress NF-κB signaling, identifying a negative feedback mechanism regulating inflammation.

items.title: SREBF2 enhances lipid metabolism and represses anti-tumor immune responses in cervical cancer by increasing ACAT2
items.author: Liang Yang
items.content: PRKN loss results in the SREBF2 degradation failure, and SREBF2 serves as a mediator for the transcriptional activation of ACAT2, leading to cholesterol accumulation and attenuated mitophagy in cervical cancer cells, thereby promoting immune evasion.

items.title: ATM Inhibition Enhances Knock-in Efficiency by Suppressing AAV-Induced Activation of Apoptotic Pathways
items.author: Yutaka Hanazono
items.content: Linear donor DNA, including AAV, overactivates ATM in a copy-number-dependent manner, causing apoptosis and shifting repair toward c-NHEJ. ATM inhibition rescues high-donor cells and suppresses c-NHEJ, thereby increasing knock-in efficiency.

items.title: Periodic confined cell migration drives partially reversible chromatin reorganization in cancer cell lines
items.author: Maria Jose Gomez-Benito
items.content: Cancer cells frequently face confined migration, causing nuclear deformation and chromatin remodeling. Microfluidics reveals partially reversible chromatin condensation, suggesting adaptive and mechanical memory strategies upon repetitive deformation.

items.title: Sleep deprivation disrupts lacrimal gland homeostasis via hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis and gut dysbiosis in mice
items.author: Zhijie Li
items.content: Sleep deprivation disrupts the HPA–gut microbiome axis, depleting SCFAs and triggering IL-17–driven lacrimal gland inflammation in mice. Pharmacological HPA inhibition and microbiome-based interventions restore immune balance and gland homeostasis.

items.title: Organic periostracum preserved in Cretaceous ammonoids from the Andean Neuquén Basin
items.author: Martín N. Rogel
items.content: Exceptional preservation of periostracum in Lower Cretaceous ammonoids collected from the foothills of the southern Andes sheds light on its morphology and chemical composition revealing that this is a very conservative structure among molluscs.

items.title: A small molecule allosterically activates SecA dependent secretion
items.author: Anastassios Economou
items.content: A small molecule that binds on SecA uncouples activation of the translocase from preprotein binding, transforming the Sec translocase into a promiscuous nanomachine that lost client selectivity and secretes any unfolded protein in vivo or in vitro.

items.title: Selective binding of divalent cations reshapes nucleosome mechanics and unlocks histone tail dynamics
items.author: Yunhui Peng
items.content: Nucleosome plasticity is critical for many chromatin-related processes. The authors demonstrate how biologically relevant divalent ions reshape nucleosome structure and mechanical stiffness.
